name: ops-core-continuity-v4-ai-proposed
goal: Advance FloorConnector operational continuity across Project Workspace, CrewBoard field handoff, and customer-safe portal trust without schema changes.
rationale: Template fallback selected high-value operational strides from current docs and wave context while preserving canonical records, project-centered workflow continuity, and human approval. The folder name is retained for generated-proposal compatibility; this is an operational-continuity wave, not an autonomous AI implementation wave.
governanceNote: Operational-first and AI-ready only. This wave must not introduce autonomous AI behavior, AI-owned operational truth, schema changes, duplicate persistence, or customer-facing actions without human approval.
productStrideCriteria: Avoids duplicate models and module silos
operationalSafeguards: Do not change schema, migrations, Supabase policies, RLS, auth, env vars, route protection, payment math, provider behavior, or unrelated business logic.

Do not add new tables, new columns, new persistence, duplicate read/write models, portal-only copies, a separate dispatch subsystem, disconnected communications, disconnected scheduling, disconnected financial models, or AI-only operational truth.
Use canonical records, tenant-safe loaders/actions, and shared read models.
Preserve project-centered workflow continuity across contractor and portal surfaces on the same canonical system.
Require human approval for customer-facing sends, signature requests, payments, scheduling commitments, provider calls, and portal visibility changes.
dependencyAndMergeSequencing: Run project-readiness-stride-v1 first; it owns the readiness language source for this wave.
crewboard-field-handoff-stride-v1 may run in parallel only if it avoids shared readiness helper rewrites and stays within schedule/job/field-handoff presentation and read-model scope.
portal-operational-trust-stride-v1 should run after project-readiness-stride-v1 where practical, or avoid redefining readiness rules.
Only one stream should edit broad docs such as docs/current-state.md at a time; other streams should narrow or defer current-state edits.
Avoid overlapping docs edits across streams and avoid changing shared prompt/template/worktree governance files from feature streams unless that governance work is explicitly assigned.
If active-waves.md or docs/current-state.md conflicts with a planning doc, preserve implemented truth and do not claim target-only capabilities are built.
mergeReadiness: Merge after validation passes, merge-check passes, the remote branch is synced, the worktree is clean, and human approval is recorded.
Open PRs as draft unless explicitly instructed otherwise.
Do not mark a PR ready until validation passes and the final stream report is complete.
If validation fails, commit only when the partial work is still useful and report the failure; otherwise stop and report.

streams.name: project-readiness-stride-v1
streams.productOutcome: Tighten Project Workspace readiness and next-action continuity over existing project, estimate, contract, job, invoice, and payment records.
fileBoundaries: Project readiness and next-action read models.
Do not sweep unrelated project, financial, schedule, portal, or docs files.
Owns this wave's shared readiness language.
streams.additionalValidationGuidance: Run focused unit/read-model tests when business shaping logic changes; if no tests are added, explain why presentation-only or existing coverage is sufficient.

streams.name: crewboard-field-handoff-stride-v1
streams.productOutcome: Improve CrewBoard to field execution handoff clarity using canonical jobs, job assignments, daily logs, field notes, and project context.
fileBoundaries: Schedule, job, and field-handoff read models.
Do not rewrite shared readiness helpers unless coordinated with project-readiness-stride-v1.
Do not sweep unrelated schedule, project, portal, or docs files.
streams.additionalValidationGuidance: Run focused unit/read-model tests when business shaping logic changes; if no tests are added, explain why presentation-only or existing coverage is sufficient.
streams.dependencyStatus: may run in parallel only if it avoids shared readiness helper rewrites; should merge after project-readiness-stride-v1 when readiness language overlaps

streams.name: portal-operational-trust-stride-v1
streams.productOutcome: Clarify customer-safe portal continuity across project, contract, invoice, payment, and shared document review paths.
fileBoundaries: Do not redefine readiness rules; consume or mirror project-readiness language only after the project stream establishes it.
Do not sweep unrelated portal, project, financial, auth, or docs files.
streams.additionalValidationGuidance: Run focused unit/read-model tests when business shaping logic changes; if no tests are added, explain why presentation-only or existing coverage is sufficient.
streams.dependencyStatus: prefer after project-readiness-stride-v1; independent only when it does not define readiness behavior
